title: Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple
tagline: Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple — spiritual town of Uttar Pradesh

heroImage.alt: Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple, Uttar Pradesh

overview.short: Shri Radha Rani Temple, is a historical Hindu temple, located in Barsana in Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh, India.
overview.description: Shri Radha Rani Temple, is a historical Hindu temple, located in Barsana in Mathura district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The temple is dedicated to the goddess Radha. The main deities of the temple are Radha Krishna who are worshiped together in the form of Shri Laadli Lal, which means the beloved daughter and son of the town. The temple is stretched on the top of Bhanugarh hills, which is about 250 meters in height.

topPlaces.name: Barsana
topPlaces.description: Barsana is a historical town and nagar panchayat in the Mathura district of the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. The town holds cultural and religious significance as the birthplace of Hindu goddess Radha, the chief consort of Krishna.

topPlaces.name: Kirti Mandir, Barsana
topPlaces.description: Kirti Mandir is a Hindu temple dedicated to Kirti Maiya, the mother of Radha Rani, located in Barsana, Uttar Pradesh, India. It is notable for enshrining Radha Rani in her childhood form, seated in the lap of her mother.

topPlaces.name: Nandgaon, Uttar Pradesh
topPlaces.description: Nandgaon is a historical town and a nagar panchayat in Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Nandgaon is a religious centre in Braj region.

items.note: Barsana is a historical town and nagar panchayat in the Mathura district of the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. The town holds cultural and religious significance as the birthplace of Hindu goddess Radha, the chief consort of Krishna.

Kirti Mandir is a Hindu temple dedicated to Kirti Maiya, the mother of Radha Rani, located in Barsana, Uttar Pradesh, India. It is notable for enshrining Radha Rani in her childhood form, seated in the lap of her mother.
Nandgaon is a historical town and a nagar panchayat in Mathura district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh. Nandgaon is a religious centre in Braj region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Agra Airport (Pandit Deen Dayal Upadhyay) (80 km) and the nearest railway station is Agra Cantt (82 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Śrī Rādhā Rānī Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Barsana, Kirti Mandir, Barsana, Nandgaon, Uttar Pradesh.
